title: Chart of accounts catalogue
description: The 14 chart-of-accounts entries the module supports. Each account documents its regulator_category (asset / liability / equity / revenue / expense), typical balance direction, default currency behaviour, and canonical use cases across the 12 transaction kinds. The ChartOfAccountsRegistry reads this catalogue at boot; the SDUI account-picker widget renders tenant-facing options; the LedgerReporter groups by regulator_category for GL exports.

accounts.description: Recognized income from invoice payments, coupon-adjusted. Every invoice-payment transaction credits revenue (typically indirectly — the immediate credit is accounts_receivable, which was previously debited when the invoice was created). Coupon credits reduce revenue.
accounts.default_currency_note: Uses tenant.base_currency. Multi-currency tenants may hold multiple currency balances simultaneously.
accounts.regulator_notes: The primary top-line revenue metric on the P&L statement. Regulators expect to see monthly + YTD totals.

accounts.description: Money owed to the tenant by customers. Debited when an invoice is created; credited when the invoice is paid (invoice_payment transaction).
accounts.regulator_notes: Balance sheet asset. High values may signal collection risk — high_watermark alerts fire when the balance exceeds tenant-configured thresholds.

accounts.description: Money the tenant currently holds in its operational cash account. Debited on invoice payments + credit refunds + chargebacks + fee charges + bank deposits + tax remittances.
accounts.regulator_notes: Balance sheet asset — critical for liquidity reporting. low_watermark alerts fire when balance falls below configured threshold.

accounts.description: Money the tenant owes to customers who requested refunds but haven't yet been paid. Debited when the refund is actually disbursed to the customer.
used_by_kinds: refund_issued (debit side, in the paired transaction)
accounts.regulator_notes: Balance sheet liability. High values may signal disbursement backlog — high_watermark alerts fire.

accounts.description: P&L expense recognizing the revenue reversal on refunds. Debited alongside a revenue credit in the adjustment transaction paired with refund_issued.
accounts.regulator_notes: P&L expense — reduces reported income. Tracked separately from write_offs because refunds have a distinct customer-service story.

accounts.description: Tax the tenant has collected from customers but hasn't yet remitted to the regulator. Debited on tax_remittance transactions.
accounts.default_currency_note: Some tenants pin this to their jurisdiction currency (e.g. EUR for EU tenants filing VAT to a EU authority).
accounts.regulator_notes: Balance sheet liability — regulator-critical. VAT report exports the tax_payable movements over a period. high_watermark alerts fire when balance exceeds configured filing threshold (indicates a filing is due).

accounts.description: Payment gateway fees (Stripe / Paddle / etc.) charged on payment captures. Debited on fee_charge transactions.
accounts.regulator_notes: P&L expense — critical for gross-margin analysis. Ops teams track this separately from other expenses to negotiate gateway rates.

accounts.description: Cash moved out of operational cash into a bank deposit account. Debited on bank_deposit transactions; credited on bank_withdrawal transactions.
accounts.regulator_notes: Balance sheet asset. Distinct from cash because deposits have different liquidity characteristics (some are locked, some are money-market with returns).

accounts.description: Uncollectible invoices written off as loss. Debited on write_off transactions.
accounts.regulator_notes: P&L expense. Regulators + auditors review this to verify appropriate collection efforts before write-off + to identify uncollectibility patterns.

accounts.description: Coupon discounts recognized as marketing expense. Debited on coupon_credit transactions.
accounts.regulator_notes: P&L expense — signals marketing efficiency. High values may indicate over-generous couponing.

accounts.description: Chargebacks (customer disputes) recognized as expense. Debited on chargeback_debit transactions.
accounts.regulator_notes: P&L expense. Regulators use this + PCI-DSS 5.4 to assess fraud + dispute rates. Sustained high values may trigger merchant-account risk reviews.

accounts.description: Money the tenant owes to third parties (vendors, suppliers, subprocessors). Credited when a bill is recognized; debited when paid.
accounts.used_by_kinds_note: Not currently used by the built-in transaction kinds. Reserved for future modules (a vendor-payables module in Wave 5+) OR manual adjustment transactions.
accounts.regulator_notes: Balance sheet liability. Ops uses this to track vendor obligations.

accounts.description: Money the tenant has paid in advance for services not yet consumed (e.g. annual insurance premiums). Debited on prepayment; credited as service is consumed.
accounts.used_by_kinds_note: Reserved for future modules. Not currently used by the built-in transaction kinds. Enterprise tenants may enable this manually for expense-accrual accounting.
accounts.regulator_notes: Balance sheet asset. Reflects future economic benefit.

accounts.description: Revenue collected in advance for services not yet delivered (e.g. an annual subscription paid upfront). Credited on payment; debited as service is delivered + revenue is recognized (per FASB ASC 606).
accounts.used_by_kinds_note: Reserved for future modules (Wave 5+ revenue-recognition automation). Enterprise tenants may enable this manually for subscription-revenue deferral accounting.
accounts.regulator_notes: Balance sheet liability. Critical for FASB ASC 606 compliance on subscription businesses. Currently manual — Wave 5+ will automate the revenue-recognition schedule.

regulator_categories.description: Balance sheet — resources the tenant owns/controls. Positive typical balance = debit. Includes: cash, accounts_receivable, bank_deposits, prepayments.
Balance sheet — obligations to third parties. Positive typical balance = credit. Includes: accounts_payable, refunds_owed, tax_payable, deferred_revenue.
Balance sheet — owner's residual claim. No accounts in this category currently (equity accounting is out-of-scope for Wave 4 SaaS-tenant use).
P&L — income recognized. Positive typical balance = credit. Includes: revenue.
P&L — costs recognized. Positive typical balance = debit. Includes: refunds_expense, gateway_fees, coupons_expense, chargebacks_expense, write_offs.

notes: The account enum is the authoritative list. Adding a new account = updating this catalogue + updating the LedgerAccount enum + updating the ChartOfAccountsRegistry + registering an #[AsLedgerAccount] class.

enabled_by_default: true = every newly-provisioned tenant gets this account in their opening_balance transaction. enabled_by_default: false = the account is opt-in (rare — reserved for accounts that only apply to specific tenant types).
The regulator_category groupings drive the GL report structure — assets + liabilities appear on the balance sheet section; revenue + expenses appear on the P&L section.

Currency behaviour: most accounts use the tenant's base_currency. tax_payable may pin to a jurisdiction currency for EU VAT tenants. cash + bank_deposits may hold balances in multiple currencies simultaneously for multi-currency tenants.

Some accounts (prepayments, deferred_revenue, accounts_payable) are declared but not USED by the built-in transaction kinds. They exist for tenants who need manual adjustment transactions + for future modules (vendor-payables, revenue-recognition automation).
